title: aster novi-belgii subsp. laevigatus
intro: Aster novi-belgii subsp. laevigatus, also known as smooth New York aster, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the northeastern United States and southeastern Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Aster novi-belgii subsp. laevigatus include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to act as an antiviral and antibacterial agent. Herbal preparations of Aster novi-belgii subsp. laevigatus can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant's roots to create a decoction, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall health.
